name: Skrymir
description: Skrymir, also known as Utgarda-Loki, is a giant from Norse mythology who appears in the Prose Edda book Gylfaginning. He is encountered by Thor, Loki, Thjalfi, and Röskva during their journey to Utgard, the stronghold of the giants. Skrymir possesses immense size and strength, but his true nature is one of illusion and deception. He tricked Thor and his companions through magical means, making them believe they were failing at tasks that were actually impossible. Skrymir's role is crucial in highlighting the vast difference in power between the gods and the giants, and showcasing the cunning and deceptive nature often associated with the giants in Norse mythology. His encounter with Thor reveals the limitations of even the strongest of the Aesir when faced with illusion and trickery. He is a symbol of the unknown and the unpredictable challenges that await heroes in the realms beyond Midgard. While seemingly benevolent at first, offering to carry their supplies, he ultimately tests the gods and reveals their flaws.
subtitle: The deceiving giant of Utgard.
appearance: Skrymir appears as a giant of enormous stature, towering over the gods. He is described as having a gruff and imposing demeanor. His physical appearance is not explicitly detailed, but his size is emphasized, making him an overwhelming presence. He carries a large sack for provisions, further highlighting his immense scale. It is implied that his true form may be different from the giant he presents himself as.
habitat: Skrymir is encountered in the wilderness between Midgard and Utgard, the realm of the giants. He is found sleeping in a vast forest, suggesting a nomadic or wandering existence within the giant realms.
behavior: Skrymir is initially helpful, offering to carry the gods' provisions. However, he constantly challenges them, making it impossible for them to open his sack or even wake him easily. He is playful and mocking, often laughing at their struggles. His behavior is ultimately deceptive, as he uses illusion to manipulate the gods' perceptions and test their abilities. He disappears without a trace after the encounter, revealing his true identity as Utgarda-Loki.
weaknesses: Trickery (His own tricks can be turned against him)

keyMyths.title: The Journey to Utgard
keyMyths.description: Thor, Loki, Thjalfi, and Röskva encounter Skrymir on their way to Utgard. He offers to carry their supplies, but makes it impossible for them to access the food within the sack.

keyMyths.title: Thor's Failed Attempts
keyMyths.description: Thor repeatedly attempts to kill Skrymir in his sleep, striking him with Mjolnir. However, Skrymir only complains about feeling a leaf or acorn falling on him, showcasing his immense durability.

keyMyths.title: The Revelation of Utgarda-Loki
keyMyths.description: Upon arriving in Utgard, the gods discover that Skrymir was actually Utgarda-Loki, the ruler of Utgard. He reveals that he used illusion to protect himself and test the gods' abilities.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Skrymir and the journey to Utgard.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Skrymir and the journey to Utgard.
